name: Tasting Experience — Rodrigo's 360 Voiceover + Marketing Narrator Scripts
subject: The canonical description of what a Comeketo tasting IS, in Rodrigo's actual voice and in the marketing narrator voice that supports it. This is a four-segment audio transcript covering: (1) a third-person narrator walking a prospect through the full tasting sequence, (2) Rodrigo introducing himself and the two booking paths, (3) a narrator credential spot referencing 500+ events, and (4) Rodrigo's own 360 tour of what the tasting looks like and why it converts. Distinctly important because the Sales Playbook tells Rodbot how to SELL the tasting — this transcript tells Rodbot what the tasting IS. Different artifact, different purpose. Every pre-tasting confirmation draft, post-tasting follow-up, and tasting-cancellation recovery message Rodbot writes depends on matching this reality.

source_context.origin: Audio transcript supplied by Jake on 2026-04-22, transcribed from video/voice recordings of Rodrigo and a marketing narrator.
source_context.format_caveat: Transcription artifacts present throughout — the transcriber misheard 'Comeketo' as 'Kamiketa' / 'Kamiketo' / 'Kamikido' / 'Comic Keto' / 'Comicado' across the four segments. Also: 'pão de quiche' is a transcription error for 'pão de queijo' (Brazilian cheese bread). These are listed in full under transcription_corrections below. Do not let the bedrock inherit the errors.
source_context.canonical_weight: HIGH for voice-specimen fidelity (segment 4 especially) — this is Rodrigo speaking about his own tasting in his own rhythm. HIGH for operational canon — this is the authoritative sequence of service. The marketing narrator segments carry lower weight for voice (they're voiceover actors or scripted copy, not Rodrigo) but are useful for understanding how Comeketo presents itself in promotional material.

segment_1_narrator_walkthrough.speaker: Third-person marketing narrator (female, per syntax — 'my favorite part of the tasting' delivered with enthusiasm)
segment_1_narrator_walkthrough.intent: Walk a prospect through the full tasting sequence end-to-end before they arrive.
extracted_sequence: Arrival — beautifully set dining area. Comeketo wants the prospect to envision their wedding day.
Check-in — prospect receives a checklist of all foods being served, so they can highlight exactly what they want to see.
Appetizers brought to the table — Brazilian coxinha, pão de queijo, empanadas, chickpea salad, 'so, so many different options.'
Salad bar buffet — prospect grabs a plate and returns to their table.
Hot sides + traditional Brazilian rodízio — hot sides brought to table, Brazilian gauchos come to the table and carve meat tableside.
Presentation during the rodízio — explains 'exactly why Comeketo catering is different than the rest.' Answers questions the prospect might have.
Brazilian desserts — flan, passion fruit mousse, grilled pineapple topped with cinnamon sugar.
Wrap-up — take a few days to think about it with whoever else is planning the event. Catering consultant follows up to create the perfect menu.
segment_1_narrator_walkthrough.close_line: 'Come to a tasting and find out for yourself.'

segment_2_rodrigo_booking_intro.speaker: Rodrigo Souza (direct first-person)
segment_2_rodrigo_booking_intro.intent: Introduce himself, thank the prospect, name the two booking paths.
segment_2_rodrigo_booking_intro.specimen_quote: Welcome! My name is Rodrigo Souza, chef and owner of Comeketo Catering. I'd like to thank you for contacting us. My understanding is that you're talking to one of our catering consultants and potentially you're going to book your event with us. There's a couple ways we can go about doing that. We have an amazing tasting that we do just to specialize for you. You can also do a consultation call where we can put a menu together over the phone with you as well.

two_booking_paths.description: The specialized, in-person path. 'Amazing tasting that we do just to specialize for you.'
Phone-based alternative. 'Put a menu together over the phone with you.'

segment_2_rodrigo_booking_intro.notable: Rodrigo opens with 'Welcome' and self-introduces with his title AND role together: 'chef and owner.' Credential and identity arrive in the same breath. Rodbot drafts that need to surface Rodrigo's authority should match this compression.

segment_3_narrator_credential_spot.speaker: Third-person marketing narrator (different register — shorter, more promotional)
segment_3_narrator_credential_spot.specimen_quote: Planning an event in 2025 is not easy, but we found a way to make it that much easier. Comeketo Catering is the go-to caterer for any and all special events this year. Having catered over 500 different events, their experience, care for detail, and care for you exceeds anyone else I've ever worked with. To learn more, join us at one of our tastings. And who knows, maybe we'll get to be a part of your special day this year.
key_claims: 500 events cataered — cross-reference with Sales Playbook's own '500 events in 15 years' social-proof move.
'Experience, care for detail, and care for you' — the three legs of the credential pitch.
Call-to-action is the tasting, not a quote or a call — consistent with the whole playbook.
segment_3_narrator_credential_spot.note: The speaker says 'anyone else I've ever worked with' — this narrator is voicing a third-party endorsement pose, not Comeketo directly. Likely a staged voiceover.

segment_4_rodrigo_360_tour.speaker: Rodrigo Souza (direct first-person — the richest voice specimen)
segment_4_rodrigo_360_tour.intent: Give a 360-degree walkthrough of the tasting: what the setup is, why it exists, what's different about it, what to expect after.
segment_4_rodrigo_360_tour.specimen_quote_full: Hello everyone, my name is Rodrigo Souza, chef and owner of Comeketo Catering. We're here today to give you a 360, what a tasting looks like, and what you're about to experience and expect. As you arrive to our facility, we have this nice setup made because we want you to envision your big day. Like some caterers that sell you a menu, here you actually get to taste, to smell, to feel, to grab. Also we get to meet the staff that's going to be at your wedding and who knows, you might get me in your wedding as well. We're able to customize menus according to your needs, according to your preference, your budget. A little bit cheesy, but we are 100% on your side because we know how much money involves to do an event like this, a wedding or a big celebration, and we want to make sure that you get something that works for you. Now, the really good thing about coming to a tasting is because usually when you do these big tastings, we like to call it, we bring some other professionals, we bring some photographers, we bring a wedding planner, we bring a decorator, we bring DJs and things like that. When you're done with our tasting, take a day or two. One of our catering consultants will get in touch with you to make sure we put a nice menu together for you with a nice package. In case you came into the tasting, please make sure you let us know — some of you guys have a special request for menus and diet restriction, all that. So it's very important that you let us know. I understand some of you guys are driving from far away but I strongly recommend you guys to make the time and come. That's gonna really dictate how comfortable you feel with the people that are going to serve your loved ones the day of your wedding, the day of your big festivity.

extracted_claims.claim: Comeketo operates out of 'our facility' — a dedicated space where tastings happen.
extracted_claims.why_matters: Cross-references the owned-venue project (slide 48) and the twice-a-week tasting cadence from the founder conversation. 'Our facility' is almost certainly the same physical space.

extracted_claims.claim: The tasting room is staged ('nice setup made') because the point is envision-ability — 'we want you to envision your big day.'
extracted_claims.why_matters: This is identical in intent to the playbook's 'paint the vision' move. The tasting room IS the vision-painter, physical and sensory.

extracted_claims.claim: The tasting is a FULL SENSORY EVENT — 'taste, smell, feel, grab' — contrasted explicitly with caterers who just 'sell you a menu.'
extracted_claims.why_matters: Rodrigo's own differentiation language. Rodbot should never describe the tasting as just 'trying the food' — it's sensory immersion that pre-sells the wedding day.

extracted_claims.claim: Prospects meet the actual event staff at the tasting.
extracted_claims.why_matters: The tasting is also a trust deposit — the couple meets the humans who will be at their wedding. Playbook-compatible (trust is the governing principle).

extracted_claims.claim: Rodrigo himself may be at the wedding — 'who knows, you might get me in your wedding as well.'
extracted_claims.why_matters: The founder sometimes works events. That's a sales lever Rodbot can't claim ('Rodrigo will be there') but can hint at ('sometimes Rodrigo works events himself').

extracted_claims.claim: Menus are customized to need, preference, budget.
extracted_claims.why_matters: Three axes of customization named explicitly. Rodbot drafts can reference all three.

extracted_claims.claim: 'A little bit cheesy, but we are 100% on your side because we know how much money involves to do an event like this.' — Rodrigo acknowledging the sales posture while stating the emotional truth.
extracted_claims.why_matters: This is a signature Rodrigo rhetorical move — self-aware, slightly self-deprecating, then sincere. The 'little bit cheesy BUT' pattern is a voice tell Rodbot should internalize but not overuse.

extracted_claims.claim: The 'big tasting' is a multi-vendor event — photographers, wedding planners, decorators, DJs are physically present.
extracted_claims.why_matters: HUGE. This is the Partnership Program operating in the room. The 'big tasting' is a co-located vendor showcase where prospects meet the whole ecosystem at once. Slide 54-68 of the Welcome deck (DJ Smokey, Above & Beyond Creatives, Ebenezer Decorations) takes on physical form at these events.

extracted_claims.claim: Post-tasting follow-up cadence: 'take a day or two' then catering consultant reaches out.
extracted_claims.why_matters: Canonical follow-up window — not same-day, not a week. Rodbot post-tasting drafts should land in the 48-72 hour window, which the playbook also supports.

extracted_claims.claim: Special request / diet restriction flagging is invited at tasting arrival.
extracted_claims.why_matters: Rodbot pre-tasting drafts should prompt for dietary restrictions — both because Rodrigo asks for it on arrival and because it gives Bibi prep time.

extracted_claims.claim: Rodrigo explicitly acknowledges the travel burden and urges prospects to come anyway.
extracted_claims.why_matters: The travel objection is pre-empted. 'I understand some of you guys are driving from far away but I strongly recommend you guys to make the time and come.' Rodbot can echo this structure when a prospect hesitates on travel — acknowledge + name the stakes.

extracted_claims.claim: The stakes frame: 'that's gonna really dictate how comfortable you feel with the people that are going to serve your loved ones the day of your wedding, the day of your big festivity.'
extracted_claims.why_matters: This is Rodrigo's actual emotional close. 'Comfort with the people who will serve your loved ones' is the emotional currency. Rodbot's peace-of-mind framing is validated AND sharpened — it's specifically comfort with the humans, not just logistics.

canonical_tasting_sequence_of_service.description: Beautifully set dining area staged as a wedding visual. Envision-ability is the point.
Prospect receives a checklist of every food being served and highlights what they want to see.
Meats and fruits. Arrival snack, social ice-breaker.
Coxinha, pão de queijo, empanadas, chickpea salad, plus additional rotating options. Brought to the table, not buffet.
Buffet-format. Prospect walks up, returns to table with a plate.

Hot sides delivered to table. Brazilian gauchos carve meat tableside (the rodízio moment). Simultaneously, a presentation is delivered explaining 'why Comeketo is different' and answering prospect questions. This is the experiential + informational peak.
Flan, passion fruit mousse (Mousse de Maracujá), grilled pineapple topped with cinnamon sugar. Closes the meal.
No on-the-spot close. Prospect leaves, thinks with the planning-partner, gets a follow-up call from a catering consultant within 48-72 hours.

appetizers.correction_note: The transcriber heard 'pão de queijo' as 'pão de quiche' — these are different things. Pão de queijo is the canonical Brazilian cheese bread; quiche is French. The dish being served is pão de queijo.

appetizers.note: Spanish/Latin American pastry rather than Portuguese — common crossover item on Brazilian catering menus.
Could be Salada de Grão-de-Bico — a small side salad often served as antepasto.

mains_and_sides.description: Brazilian churrasco meats carved tableside by gauchos. Specific cuts not named in this transcript — cross-reference menu_catalog_bibi_prep_list.json for the full cut list.
Not enumerated in this transcript. Cross-reference menu_catalog_bibi_prep_list.json — includes Feijão Tropeiro, rice, farofa, etc.

desserts.transcript_form: grilled pineapple topped with cinnamon sugar
desserts.note: Abacaxi grelhado com açúcar e canela in Brazilian tradition.

the_big_tasting_vendor_co_location.finding: The 'big tasting' format is a multi-vendor showcase, not a food-only event. Rodrigo's own language: 'we bring some other professionals, we bring some photographers, we bring a wedding planner, we bring a decorator, we bring DJs and things like that.'
the_big_tasting_vendor_co_location.cross_reference_to_partnership_program: This is the Partnership Program operating in physical space. The tier-1-through-tier-4 framework from knowledge/partnership_program.json gets enacted at the big tasting — partners who are at or near Preferred/Growth tiers show up physically and get exposure to Comeketo's converting pipeline.
the_big_tasting_vendor_co_location.cross_reference_to_welcome_deck_vendors: Slide 54-68 of the Welcome-Rodrigo deck names DJ Smokey, Above & Beyond Creatives (photographer), Ebenezer Decorations. These are almost certainly the vendors brought into the big tasting. The deck's 'Partner Network Reveal' arc isn't just a pitch — it's a preview of who the prospect will meet at the tasting.
the_big_tasting_vendor_co_location.cross_reference_to_owned_venue: The 'big tasting' with multiple vendors presupposes a venue big enough to host food service + photo display + decorator presentation + DJ setup + a sizeable group of prospects. That venue is very likely the 'our facility' Rodrigo names, which is very likely the slide-48 owned-venue ('THIS VERY VENUE — Officially Opens this Spring, 50-100 people'). Three data points now line up.
the_big_tasting_vendor_co_location.cadence_implication: Founder conversation: 'tastings happen twice a week.' This transcript: 'usually when you do these BIG tastings' — the phrasing suggests not every tasting is a big tasting. Some may be smaller (individual couples), some are the big multi-vendor showcase format. Rodbot should not assume every tasting is a multi-vendor event unless confirmed.

rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.specimen: 'A little bit cheesy, but we are 100% on your side because...'
rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.rodbot_application: Use sparingly for moments where Rodbot is about to make a claim that could sound salesy. Acknowledge the sales-adjacency, then state the sincere truth. Do not quote the phrase verbatim — match the shape.

rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.specimen: 'Like some caterers that sell you a menu, here you actually get to taste, to smell, to feel, to grab.'
rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.rodbot_application: When distinguishing the tasting from a menu PDF or phone consult, list the sensory verbs. Rodbot can echo the contrast pattern — 'others do X; we do Y' — without being smug about it.

rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.specimen: 'We get to meet the staff that's going to be at your wedding.'
rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.rodbot_application: Post-tasting drafts can name WHO the couple met. 'You met Bibi — she'll run your kitchen the day of.' Continuity of faces is a trust deposit, and Rodbot should carry forward who was introduced.

rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.specimen: 'We know how much money involves to do an event like this... we want to make sure that you get something that works for you.'
rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.rodbot_application: When money comes up, Rodbot should not pitch value — Rodbot should acknowledge the magnitude and align with the prospect's interest. 'Your interest' not 'our package.'

rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.specimen: 'I understand some of you guys are driving from far away but I strongly recommend you guys to make the time and come. That's gonna really dictate how comfortable you feel with the people that are going to serve your loved ones.'
rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.rodbot_application: When a prospect hesitates on travel to the tasting, Rodbot's move: acknowledge the distance, then reframe around the stakes (comfort with the humans who will serve your loved ones). Never drop the ask.

rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.pattern: Emotional close framing — 'the people serving your loved ones'
rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.specimen: 'the people that are going to serve your loved ones the day of your wedding, the day of your big festivity.'
rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.rodbot_application: This phrase is the emotional core of the B2C register. Internalize the noun-structure: 'your loved ones' (not 'your guests'), 'festivity' (not 'event'). Do NOT quote verbatim — use it as directional.

rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.specimen: 'Welcome! My name is Rodrigo Souza, chef and owner of Comeketo Catering.'
rodrigo_voice_specimens_for_rodbot.rodbot_application: When introducing Rodrigo (not impersonating), Rodbot names both roles together — chef AND owner. The dual credential IS the authority structure.

dish_name_misspellings.note: Critical — 'quiche' is French, 'queijo' is Portuguese. Do not propagate the error anywhere downstream.

transcription_corrections.note: The audio transcriber had no Portuguese vocabulary and an apparently American ear — all five company-name variants are phonetically plausible mishearings of 'Comeketo.' Do not use any of these forms as canonical anywhere in the bedrock.

cross_references_and_confirmations.confirms: Tastings happen at a physical facility owned or controlled by Comeketo ('our facility').
cross_references_and_confirmations.anchor: knowledge/business_topology_from_founder_conversation.json point 20 (twice-a-week at a hall), projects/comeketo_owned_venue.json (slide-48 'THIS VERY VENUE'). Three independent data points now converge on: Comeketo operates a physical hall that is simultaneously the tasting venue, the owned venue being promoted at tasting, and the location where multi-vendor big tastings happen.

cross_references_and_confirmations.confirms: 500+ events over 15 years as the credential anchor.
cross_references_and_confirmations.anchor: knowledge/sales_playbook_v2.json signature social proof ('500 events in 15 years'). The marketing narrator says '500 different events.' Consistent.

cross_references_and_confirmations.confirms: The Partnership Program operates physically at big tastings — photographers, planners, decorators, DJs are in the room.
cross_references_and_confirmations.anchor: knowledge/partnership_program.json (tiered framework), knowledge/brand_welcome_rodrigo.json (DJ Smokey, Above & Beyond, Ebenezer). The transcript upgrades the partnership program from abstract framework to described physical practice.

cross_references_and_confirmations.confirms: Post-tasting follow-up in the 48-72 hour window ('take a day or two').
cross_references_and_confirmations.anchor: knowledge/sales_playbook_v2.json cadence section + post-tasting follow-up sequence. Rodrigo's own words match the playbook. Rodbot post-tasting drafts should respect this window.
knowledge/brand_wedding_experience.json + the 4-Secrets framing in the Welcome-Rodrigo deck. The 'we don't just sell you a menu' contrast is a recurring Rodrigo rhetorical move.

cross_references_and_confirmations.new_fact_vs_bedrock: Explicit naming of the four vendor types at the big tasting: photographers, wedding planners, decorators, DJs.
cross_references_and_confirmations.implication: The Partnership Program tiers should prioritize these four vendor categories. If a fifth type (florist, rental, videographer) isn't surfaced at the tasting, tier cultivation for them is lower-priority.

cross_references_and_confirmations.new_fact_vs_bedrock: The tasting sequence of service is canonically: arrival → checklist → cheese board → appetizers → salad bar → hot sides + rodízio + presentation → desserts → wrap-up.
cross_references_and_confirmations.implication: Rodbot pre-tasting drafts can reference specific moments ('you'll have the rodízio served tableside'). Post-tasting drafts can name specific moments the couple actually experienced.

cross_references_and_confirmations.new_fact_vs_bedrock: Rodrigo himself may work specific weddings ('you might get me in your wedding as well').
cross_references_and_confirmations.implication: The founder is sometimes present at events. Rodbot should never promise this, but can mention it as a possibility when a couple is choosing between signing and walking.

additions_to_character_md: Add the 'Sensory contrast' pattern to B2C signature moves — 'others sell you a menu; we let you taste, smell, feel, grab.'
Add the 'Travel-burden acknowledgment + stakes reframe' pattern — when a prospect cites distance, Rodbot acknowledges AND reframes around comfort with the humans who will serve their loved ones.
Add 'your loved ones' and 'your big festivity' to the B2C lexicon. These are Rodrigo's own nouns. More intimate than 'your guests' and 'your event.' Use sparingly — they're high-weight.
Add the 'A little bit cheesy, but...' self-aware acknowledgment pattern. Low-frequency but powerful when a Rodbot draft is approaching sales-adjacent territory and needs to land with sincerity.
post_tasting_draft_templates: Rodbot can reference specific tasting moments by name: 'I know the rodízio was served to your table — Bibi's gauchos are one of the quieter wow moments.'
Rodbot can reference specific dishes the couple gravitated to, if captured: 'You mentioned the passion fruit mousse — easy to build a dessert station around that.'
Rodbot can reference the vendor partners the couple met: 'You met DJ Smokey at the tasting — his package and ours work cleanly together. Want me to loop him in on the scheduling conversation?'

Rodbot should NEVER draft post-tasting messages that default to '48 hours after tasting.' Rodrigo's own words are 'take a day or two' — Rodbot should respect the 48-72 hour window as canon and not hurry it.
pre_tasting_draft_templates: Rodbot can ask about dietary restrictions explicitly before arrival — matches Rodrigo's own invitation ('some of you guys have a special request for menus and diet restriction').
Rodbot can set vision-expectations — 'the room is staged so you can envision your day, not just try the food.'
When a couple hesitates on travel to the tasting, Rodbot reaches for Rodrigo's own travel-reframe — stakes before pressure.
what_rodbot_must_not_do: Never quote 'A little bit cheesy, but we are 100% on your side' verbatim — that's Rodrigo's phrasing, not Rodbot's. Use the pattern, not the line.
Never promise Rodrigo will be at a specific wedding — that's his own qualified statement ('you might get me in your wedding'), not a guarantee.

Never describe the tasting as 'just food tasting.' The transcript makes clear it's sensory + partnership preview + staff introduction + envision-the-day + presentation. Flattening it to food misses the whole value prop.
Never use 'pão de quiche.' It's 'pão de queijo.'

operational_implications: The 'our facility' question is now tighter. The facility hosts twice-a-week tastings AND multi-vendor big tastings AND is likely the slide-48 owned venue. projects/comeketo_owned_venue.json should be updated to reflect this triangulation — the venue is very likely already operational as the tasting space, and the slide-48 'Officially Opens this Spring' language may refer to the hall's public-bookings launch rather than the physical space itself.

The co-located vendor pattern at big tastings means the Partnership Program tier-building has a physical qualifier — Preferred+ partners are the ones invited to the big tasting. Rodbot-drafted partner outreach should implicitly understand that tier graduation unlocks tasting-event inclusion.

The tasting checklist (step 2 of the sequence) is an artifact Rodbot doesn't see yet. It's handed to prospects at arrival. Flag for raw-data pull — the checklist content is a useful reference for what dishes are currently on the tasting rotation.

The presentation delivered during the rodízio (step 6) is almost certainly the Welcome-Rodrigo deck's content in live form. The deck we've indexed may be the slide version; the live presentation may be freestyle Rodrigo around the same beats. Worth confirming.

Rodrigo invites diet-restriction and special-request disclosure at tasting arrival. Rodbot's pre-tasting confirmation drafts should proactively ask for this — doing so earlier gives Bibi more prep time than arrival-day disclosure.
notes: This file preserves Rodrigo's actual speech specimens verbatim because voice fidelity matters for Rodbot training. The specimen_quote_full field in segment_4 is the full unedited Rodrigo tour — do not abridge it in downstream summarization.
The marketing narrator segments (1 and 3) are useful for understanding how Comeketo presents itself, but carry lower weight for Rodbot voice — they are scripted voiceover, not Rodrigo.
The sequence of service captured here should be treated as CANON until contradicted. If Bibi or Rodrigo describe a different sequence, update this file — but do not let bedrock drift silently.

Consider graduating the tasting sequence of service into its own knowledge card later (knowledge/tasting_sequence_of_service.json) if Rodbot starts surfacing it frequently. For now, keeping it inline here preserves the context.
The transcription corrections section is load-bearing — any downstream file that pulls from this one MUST pull the corrected forms, not the transcript forms.

sources.diff: Initial creation from an audio transcript Jake supplied. Four segments preserved: (1) narrator walkthrough, (2) Rodrigo booking intro, (3) narrator credential spot, (4) Rodrigo 360 tour. Extracted canonical 8-step sequence of service, cataloged specific dishes named, surfaced the multi-vendor 'big tasting' pattern with cross-references to the Partnership Program and the owned-venue project, and captured 7 Rodrigo voice specimens with rodbot-application notes. Listed all 5 company-name transcription errors and the 'pão de quiche' correction. Added rodbot_voice_implications section with proposed character.md additions and pre/post-tasting draft templates.
